text: This is our surgical technique for laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass.
The surgical setup includes six laparoscopic ports positioned to facilitate the use of two hands by both the surgeon and the first assistant during the gastric bypass procedure.
The Roux-en-Y gastric bypass is done in the anticholic-antigastric approach, which simplifies the operative procedure.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to divide the greater omentum, reducing tension on the Roux limb during the laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass procedure.
